problem.title: Search in Rotated Array
problem.description: There is an integer array nums sorted in ascending order (with distinct values).
assumptions: Originally sorted, then rotated at some pivot.

example.explanation: Array rotated at index 4. Binary search: determine which half is sorted, check if target in that half.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Binary Search
solutions.description: At each step, one half of the rotated array is always sorted; determine which half is sorted, then check whether the target falls in that range to decide where to recurse. This keeps the search space halving at every step despite the rotation, maintaining O(log n) time. The key insight is that comparing nums[left] to nums[mid] tells you unambiguously which side is contiguous.
